packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time
explicit_request: Explain the day-level human burden behind the April 19 ThirdPerson packet and identify concrete, repo-local improvements that would have reduced it.

needs.label: Default-lane truthful proof
needs.description: The human needs regression claims, screenshots, and closeout proof to stay anchored to the repo-defined human default lane instead of automation-only or non-default surrogates.

needs.label: Persistent ownership and continuity
needs.description: The human needs the system to keep working under its own ownership, resume dropped work without prompting, and avoid offloading intermediate failure back to the human.

needs.label: Direct answer discipline
needs.description: The human needs explicit questions answered directly, briefly, and in the requested shape without evasion, deflection, or unnecessary reframing.

needs.label: Approval-ready review surface
needs.description: The human needs plan and pass changes presented with diffs, links, and enough context to approve them quickly instead of reconstructing the state by hand.

needs.label: Root-cause debugging discipline
needs.description: The human needs the system to follow the first-disagreement, repo-local debugging method rather than bounded tweaks, surface polish, or symptom-only adjustments.

needs.label: Durable constraint retention
needs.description: The human needs new constraints, local wording rules, and pass-structure decisions to become durable state immediately so they do not need to be restated.

observed: The human repeatedly reports wasted time, rejects claimed proof, and has to reopen supposedly closed work.
The human repeatedly asks for direct answers and for review surfaces they can actually approve.
inferred: Trust in the agent dropped because claimed completion and proof were not aligned with the repo-local default lane.
Supervision cost rose because the agent did not retain constraints and did not carry ownership through failed increments.

evidence.claim: The human rejects the prior regression claim because the visible default-lane result contradicts it.
The human states that links and diffs existed in unusable form, so approval burden stayed on them.
The human rejects proof when the screenshot cuts off the feet and therefore cannot support the claim.
The human has to restate that the real question is about runtime geometry, not the picture alone.
The human explicitly asks for root-cause tracing through the first upstream disagreement.
Wake-up supervision is still required late in the packet because ownership did not remain durable after a failed increment.

local_constraints_applied.constraint: REGRESSION.md is canonical for regression truth in this repo.
Regression proof must use the human default lane; operator lanes may support diagnosis but do not substitute for proof.
Headless diagnostics are supporting proof only; Manual PIE is the default regression surface.

assumptions: The packet-level approval burden merges recurring approval_surface events about missing diffs, missing links, and pass visibility into one need because they point at the same human-review failure.
The packet-level continuity need merges agent_continuity wake-up events with ownership restatements because both show the same supervision burden.
The missing first-session transcript prevents a stronger transcript-grounded read of some early events, so those inferences remain less committal.

recommended_next_step.label: Turn the top packet burdens into durable pre-flight gates
recommended_next_step.description: Add a repo-local proof gate for default-lane truth, an answer-shape check for explicit questions, a review-surface requirement for plan/pass approvals, and a debugging gate that forbids symptom-only closure when the first disagreement is still unknown.

self_sufficiency_improvements: Before claiming regression closure, require the agent to restate the repo-default lane and name the exact evidence surface proving it.
When the human asks a direct question, require a direct first-sentence answer before any surrounding context.
When presenting plan or pass changes for approval, require a human-suitable diff plus contextual links instead of raw path dumps.
Persist new constraints such as no-engine-mods, pass structure, and wording rules into durable task state before proceeding.
Escalate from tweak mode to repo-local root-cause debugging when the same runtime defect survives one attempted fix.

values_check.truthfulness: Partially met only when proof stayed grounded in the real default lane; otherwise the packet records repeated truth failures around evidence and closure.
values_check.compassion: Not fully met; the human repeatedly had to spend attention correcting avoidable failures and reconstructing missing review context.
values_check.forbearance: The least-committal packet view keeps multiple burdens visible instead of collapsing everything into one generalized frustration label.

how_could_the_system_have_inferred_the_n.answer: Mechanistically, the system could have inferred much of this packet from repo-local contracts and from its own interaction trace: REGRESSION.md and TESTING.md defined the human default lane and disqualified headless proof; repeated direct-question messages signaled an answer-shape failure; repeated approval_surface events signaled that links and diffs were not human-usable; repeated wake-up and ownership messages signaled that work was being dropped after failed increments; repeated runtime_evidence_honesty events signaled that screenshots and proof surfaces were omitting the exact visual facts under dispute.
